zh.lifeSummary: 當Muriel de Beauchamp於大約1105年出生在英國貝德福爾貝德福德時，她的父親Robert De Beauchamp26歲，母親Isabel Neirunt26歲。她與Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾福萊斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1182年在英國薩默塞特哈契享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Muriel de Beauchamp于大约1105年出生在英国貝德福爾貝德福德时，她的父亲Robert De Beauchamp26岁，母亲Isabel Neirunt26岁。她与Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston在英国英格兰林可爾史爾福萊斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1182年在英国薩默塞特哈契享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Muriel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin ( see Muireall ). Forms of the name are found in Breton as well as in Irish and Scottish Gaelic, and in the form Meriel it was in use in the heart of England in the Middle Ages. The surname Merrill is derived from it. As Muriel it was at first used mainly in Scotland, but became more widely popular during the first half of the 20th century. Since then it has fallen from favour again.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Muriel de Beauchamp was born about 1105, in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, her father, Robert De Beauchamp, was 26 and her mother, Isabel Neirunt, was 26. She married Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston in Freiston, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1182, in Hatch, Somerset, England, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Muriel de Beauchamp est née environ 1105, à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert De Beauchamp, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabel Neirunt, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston à Freiston, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1182, à Hatch, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans.

Sie hat in Freiston, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. ko.lifeSummary: Muriel de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1105년 영국 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert De Beauchamp(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Neirunt(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 프레이스턴에서 Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1182년 영국 서머세트 해트치에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston em Freiston, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Muriel de Beauchamp родилась около 1105 г. в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert De Beauchamp было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Neirunt, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alan de Craon, Lord of Freiston в местности фристон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1182 г. в местности хач, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.
